title: Count Good Triplets
content:
Given an array of integers arr, and three integers a, b and c. You need to find the number of good triplets.

A triplet (arr[i], arr[j], arr[k]) is good if the following conditions are true:

• 0 <= i < j < k < arr.length

• |arr[i] - arr[j]| <= a

• |arr[j] - arr[k]| <= b

• |arr[i] - arr[k]| <= c

Where |x| denotes the absolute value of x.

Return the number of good triplets.

Example 1:

Input: arr = [3,0,1,1,9,7], a = 7, b = 2, c = 3
Output: 4
Explanation: There are 4 good triplets: [(3,0,1), (3,0,1), (3,1,1), (0,1,1)].

Example 2:

Input: arr = [1,1,2,2,3], a = 0, b = 0, c = 1
Output: 0
Explanation: No triplet satisfies all conditions.

Constraints:

• 3 <= arr.length <= 100

• 0 <= arr[i] <= 1000

• 0 <= a, b, c <= 1000

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution(object):
    def countGoodTriplets(self, arr, a, b, c):

        :type arr: List[int]
        :type a: int
        :type b: int
        :type c: int
        :rtype: int

hints: Notice that the constraints are small enough for a brute force solution to pass.
Loop through all triplets, and count the ones that are good.

solutions.explanation:
Triple nested loop checking all conditions for triplet validity.
Time: O(n³)
Space: O(1)
